title: Viola samothracica
intro: Viola samothracica, also known as Samothracian pansy or Samothraki pansy, is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae, native to the island of Samothraki in Greece. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Viola samothracica include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Viola samothracica include infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and to soothe digestive issues.
taxonomy: Viola samothracica is a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It belongs to the genus Viola, which comprises about 600 species of plants. The genus Viola is further classified under the order Malpighiales and the family Violaceae.
